channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why supporting a shelter for women is now 'kind of radioactive'
item.description: That's how researcher Beatriz Garcia Nice describes the new U.S. stance under the Trump administration to programs addressing gender-based violence.
item.content:encoded: That's how researcher Beatriz Garcia Nice describes the new U.S. stance under the Trump administration to programs addressing gender-based violence.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Trump's new reasoning for Iran strikes. And, takeaways from the first 2026 primaries
item.description: President Trump offers new reasoning for the U.S. attack on Iran. And, results from the first midterm primary of 2026 are in, providing an outlook for the matchups for key Senate seats.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump offers new reasoning for the U.S. attack on Iran. And, results from the first midterm primary of 2026 are in, providing an outlook for the matchups for key Senate seats.

(Image credit: -/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Telehealth abortion is in the courts. Share your experience.
item.description: Mifepristone is facing another major legal challenge.
item.content:encoded:
Mifepristone is facing another major legal challenge.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Strikes resume on Iran, U.S. military says Tehran's defenses 'severely degraded'
item.description: Israel's military said it had begun a "broad wave of strikes" in Tehran Wednesday morning. U.S. officials touted early gains, while Democrats warned the war could widen.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's military said it had begun a "broad wave of strikes" in Tehran Wednesday morning. U.S. officials touted early gains, while Democrats warned the war could widen.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: ICE has spun a massive surveillance web. We talked to people caught in it
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security, which oversees ICE and Border Patrol, is using a broad web of surveillance tools — purchased as its budget has ballooned under this administration — to monitor, apprehend and intimidate the people it seeks to deport and the U.S. citizens critical of its policies.
item.content:encoded: The Department of Homeland Security, which oversees ICE and Border Patrol, is using a broad web of surveillance tools — purchased as its budget has ballooned under this administration — to monitor, apprehend and intimidate the people it seeks to deport and the U.S. citizens critical of its policies.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: America has a housing affordability crisis. Building houses for rent can help
item.description: Developers are building more single-family houses for renting. That can lower prices for both renters and buyers.
item.content:encoded:
Developers are building more single-family houses for renting. That can lower prices for both renters and buyers.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: As Paralympics approach, U.S. skier Sydney Peterson balances training and research
item.description: Sydney Peterson is among the U.S. athletes heading to the 2026 Winter Paralympics. A neuroscientist in training, Peterson is studying movement disorders, similar to her own condition.
item.content:encoded:
Sydney Peterson is among the U.S. athletes heading to the 2026 Winter Paralympics. A neuroscientist in training, Peterson is studying movement disorders, similar to her own condition.

(Image credit: Emily Chen-Newton)
item.dc:creator: Emily Chen-Newton

item.title: On the brink of death, a woman is saved by a stranger and his family
item.description: In 1982, Jean Muenchrath and her boyfriend went mountaineering in California's Sierra Nevada. Then, an unexpected storm veered them dangerously off course. Luckily, their unsung hero found them.
item.content:encoded: In 1982, Jean Muenchrath and her boyfriend went mountaineering in California's Sierra Nevada. Then, an unexpected storm veered them dangerously off course. Luckily, their unsung hero found them.
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: 5 takeaways from the first primaries of the 2026 midterms
item.description: Voters headed to the polls Tuesday in the first primaries of the midterm election season. The latest results from Arkansas, North Carolina and Texas.
item.content:encoded:
Voters headed to the polls Tuesday in the first primaries of the midterm election season. The latest results from Arkansas, North Carolina and Texas.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: North Carolina Rep. Valerie Foushee holds narrow lead over challenger Nida Allam
item.description: The race is too close to call between Democratic Rep. Valerie Foushee and her anti-establishment challenger Nida Allam in North Carolina's 4th congressional district and is likely headed to a recount.
item.content:encoded:
The race is too close to call between Democratic Rep. Valerie Foushee and her anti-establishment challenger Nida Allam in North Carolina's 4th congressional district and is likely headed to a recount.

(Image credit: Jonathan Drake/Reuters; Andrew Harnik/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore
